nativeRange.habitat: The Acei Cichlid is endemic to Lake Malawi, occurring along the western shoreline at sites including Nkhata Bay, Senga Bay, Ngara, Ruarwe, Bandawe, Karonga, and the area around the South Rukuru River. It lives at the transition zone between rocky shorelines and sandy substrate, and is unusual among mbuna — the rock-dwelling cichlids of Lake Malawi — for its habit of grazing algae from submerged logs and waterlogged driftwood rather than from bare rock.
nativeRange.biotope: Lake Malawi rocky-sandy transition zone with scattered submerged wood

decorPreferences: Stacked rockwork with caves and territories
Submerged driftwood/bogwood for epixylic algae grazing
Open mid- and upper-water swimming space

diet.notes: Predominantly grazes algae growing on submerged wood and rocks in the wild. In captivity is generally advised to feed a spirulina-based flake or pellet, blanched green vegetables (spinach, courgette), and nori, with only occasional protein supplementation. Excess animal protein has been associated with Malawi bloat in mbuna.

avoidWith: Highly aggressive mbuna (Melanochromis auratus, Maylandia lombardoi 'Kenyi')
Other Acei colour variants (yellow-tail with white-tail) — hybridisation risk

summary: Peaceful-for-a-mbuna Lake Malawi cichlid that uniquely grazes algae from submerged driftwood. Generally considered one of the more beginner-friendly African cichlids when kept in a large group.
careNotes: The Acei Cichlid has not yet received a formal scientific description, so its genus placement remains provisional; the name Mchenga acei has been proposed as a future reclassification. Several geographic color forms exist — most commonly the yellow-tail 'Ngara' and the white-tail 'Msuli/Itungi' variants — and these should not be mixed in one tank because they readily hybridize with each other. Generally regarded as one of the most peaceful mbuna, the Acei Cichlid still shows the territorial behavior typical of the group: adult males will spar, and keeping a group of six or more (ideally one male to three or four females) helps distribute that aggression across the tank. Unlike most mbuna, this species tends to use the middle and upper water column, so vertical swimming room is appreciated. It is susceptible to Malawi bloat — a serious digestive condition linked to excess animal protein — so a spirulina and vegetable-led diet is generally advised.
breedingNotes: The Acei Cichlid is a maternal mouthbrooder — the female incubates eggs inside her mouth rather than in a nest. Females typically carry 20 to 50 eggs or fry for roughly three to four weeks before releasing free-swimming juveniles. Breeding has been reported as fairly straightforward in well-conditioned groups kept at a female-skewed sex ratio (one male to several females). A separate grow-out tank is generally advised, as adults and tankmates will predate on released fry.

fish.breedingNotes: A maternal mouthbrooder that incubates eggs and fry inside the female's mouth for roughly three to four weeks, typically producing 20 to 50 juveniles per spawn.
